ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: My friends expected me to be constantly available online.
For my friends, it was important that I was constantly available online.
Most of my friends approved of me being constantly available online.
I felt a social obligation to be constantly available online.
I was nervous about how people would respond to my posts and photos.
I felt anxious about how others would respond when I shared a new photo on social media.
I felt nervous after I shared a post or photo to see how others responded to it.
I felt nervous about how others would respond when I posted new updates on social media.
I put a lot of effort into finding or creating a photo that others would approve of when I posted it online.
I put a lot of effort into composing messages and posts I shared online.
I feared my friends were having more rewarding experiences than me.
I feared that others had more rewarding experiences than me.
I got worried when I found out my friends were having fun without me.
I got anxious when I didn't know what my friends were up to.
I had to check too many notifications.
I felt overwhelmed with the flow of messages/notifications on my phone.
It felt like there was always a reminder, like a flashing light or buzz, that there was some other message that I needed to attend to.
I felt stressed because I had to sift through a lot of unimportant notifications to get to the important ones.
On top of the other things I had to do, keeping up with notifications was a chore.
I spent too much time responding to notifications/messages.
I had to have my phone with me to know what was going on.
I felt lost or ''naked'' without my phone.
I was constantly checking my phone for messages/notifications.
I felt socially unavailable when I did not have my phone.
